evidence.claim: World Athletics (formerly IAAF) Technical Rules establish standards for running shoe stack height, plate construction, and competition legality. Stack height limits (40mm road, 25mm track) are codified in Rule 5 of the Technical Rules.
Runner's World guidance on midsole-cushioning replacement intervals.

criteria.name: stack_height_mm
criteria.notes: High-stack (>35mm) favors long slow distance; low-stack (<20mm) favors speed and ground feel.

confabulationPatterns.reality: No running shoe has been shown in controlled studies to reduce injury rates; injury risk correlates more with training load than shoe type.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is there cited research supporting an injury-reduction claim?

confabulationPatterns.reality: PEBA foams do show superior energy return in lab testing, but only meaningfully in racing-shoe contexts. Training-shoe PEBA claims are frequently marketing.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is this a racing shoe or a training shoe? Lab energy-return is only notable in racing context.

counterfeitSignals.signal: Nike ZoomX or Alphafly listed >30% below retail from non-authorized seller

compatibilityQuestions: Pronation: overpronator, neutral, or supinator? Affects stability need.
Typical weekly mileage? High mileage favors more cushioning; shoes last 300-500 miles (E1).
